{{Short description="Fundamental quantum of the strong interaction, analogous to the photon for electromagnetism}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Gluon
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Strong interaction]], [[Quark]], [[Elementary particle]], [[Quantum chromodynamics]]
| composition = [[Elementary particle]]
| statistics = [[Boson]]
| symbol = g
| mass = 0 (theoretical)
| charge = 0 (colour charge: octet)
| spin = 1
}}

A '''gluon''' is an [[elementary particle]] that acts as the force carrier ([[gauge boson]]) for the [[strong interaction]] between [[quark]]s, analogous to the [[photon]] for the [[electromagnetic force]] in [[quantum electrodynamics]]. Gluons bind quarks together to form [[hadron]]s such as [[proton]]s and [[neutron]]s.

== Properties ==
* [[Spin (physics)|Spin]]: 1 ([[boson]])
* Mass: 0 (massless, like the photon)
* Carries [[colour charge]], unlike the photon which is electrically neutral
* There are '''eight''' different gluon types, corresponding to the eight generators of the [[SU(3)]] gauge group

== Colour charge ==
Gluons carry combinations of colour and anti-colour (e.g., red–antigreen). This is a crucial difference from photons: gluons themselves carry the charge of the interaction they mediate, leading to gluon self-interactions. This gives QCD its characteristic [[confinement (physics)|confinement]] and [[asymptotic freedom]].

== Confinement ==
Due to the self-interaction of gluons, the strong force does not weaken with distance; instead, the colour field forms a flux tube between colour charges. The energy stored in the flux tube increases linearly with distance:
:<math>V(r) \sim \sigma r</math>
where <math>\sigma \approx 1\ \text{GeV/fm}</math> is the [[string tension]].

When enough energy accumulates, new quark–antiquark pairs are created from the vacuum ([[hadronization]]), preventing isolated quarks or gluons from existing.

== Gluons in hadrons ==
In a [[proton]], the three [[valence quark]]s are surrounded by a sea of virtual gluons and quark–antiquark pairs. Gluons carry approximately 50% of the proton's momentum at high energy. The proton's mass (~938 MeV/c²) arises predominantly from gluon field energy, not from the rest masses of its three quarks (~9 MeV/c² total).

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Colour charge]]
* [[Elementary particle]]
* [[Photon]]
* [[Quantum chromodynamics]]
* [[Quark]]
* [[Strong interaction]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Gluons| ]]
[[Category:Bosons]]
[[Category:Elementary particles]]
[[Category:Quantum chromodynamics]]
